dismissalText.commentary: Priyansh Arya c Marsh b Siddharth 93 (37b 4x4 9x6 72m) SR: 251.35

dismissalComment.html: Priyansh is livid, yells as Marsh seems to have taken an ice-cool boundary catch at long-off. Sent upstairs by the ump. Catch taken above head as he lifted both feet, the back foot then landed... and we'll need to zoom in for this... inches within the cushion! Priyansh walks back to a rousing occasion from fans, team-mates and owners. Was a hittable slot ball angling away around off, Priyansh cleared the front leg and swung with the bat-face opening slightly. Nearly enough. He'll leave without a hundred

dismissalText.commentary: Cooper Connolly c Siddharth b Prince Yadav 87 (46b 8x4 7x6 64m) SR: 189.13

dismissalComment.html: Is the party finally over? Yes! Prince prays likely in relief. Connolly looks up, sighs and walks back. Job done though. Was a 139ks pace-on, back of length ball just outside off, rose around rib-height, Connolly looked to belt it through midwicket but got a top edge that was taken at short third. Partnership ends at 182 off 80

dismissalText.commentary: Shreyas Iyer c Mohammed Shami b Mohsin Khan 5 (6b 0x4 0x6 9m) SR: 83.33

dismissalComment.html: Three quick wickets before PBKS reach 200. 127ks, angling away again. Shreyas looked to drive the fullish length ball inside out over extra cover again. He's a touch too late on it this time and outside edges it to short third. Impressive from Mohsin as that was slow but still hurried the batter

dismissalText.commentary: Marcus Stoinis run out (Pooran/†Pant) 29 (16b 2x4 2x6 36m) SR: 181.25

dismissalComment.html: Stunnig bowling! Near yorker. 137ks low full toss on leg, flicked through midwicket. Pooran ran in and threw it on the bounce over leg stump. Pant whips off the bail... no, it was a direct hit replay shows. Five off the last over, effectively a maiden

dismissalText.commentary: Rishabh Pant c †Prabhsimran Singh b Arshdeep Singh 43 (23b 1x4 4x6 36m) SR: 186.95

dismissalComment.html: Keeper catch. Given. Pant chats with Pooran and... reviews. Yorker landing just outside the crease around sixth stump. Pant's knee was on the ground and facing cover as he reached out, opened the bat face and looked to slice it. Ball was too low, Pant's grip weakened and outside edge hit the ground with the ball close to bat. Hard to tell what it hit from the audio... but what about sight? It does lead to a conclusive answer as there is a deflection away. Easy catch for the keeper. Arshdeep's finger went up straight away
